title: [HLS] Initial commit for HLS dialect and HLS attributes
body:
This commit introduces a new dialect, the HLS dialect. The HLS dialect is intended to be a parent dialect for transformations, analyses and attributes which exist within the context of a high-level synthesis flow. I do not foresee that this dialect will introduce any new operations.

The first use of the dialect is the implementation of HLS dialect attributes, which intend to capture similar functionality as pragma directives in standard HLS tools. HLS dialect attributes are defined in two steps:
- First, a directive implementation is defined in TableGen. This may be any attribute, but most directives will map to `StructAttr`'s
- Then, the TableGen'erated directive is wrapped to implement a dialect attribute.

By wrapping a TableGen'erated attribute into a dialect attribute we force the parser to delegate parsing and printing to the dialect. Through this, we may instantiate the actual TableGen'erated attribute, rather than letting the parser default to parsing a dictionary. The motivation for doing so is to have more type-safety and validation around struct-like attributes, instead of relying on dictionaries, as well as being able to round-trip the HLS directives.

HLS directives are currently able to be placed on any operation which takes an attribute dictionary. If we see the need for it, we may extend the `HLSDirective` interface to support i.e. validation, ensuring that some directives are restricted to specific operations.

Two example attributes are included in this commit to showcase the functionality; the _pipeline_ and _function_instantiate_ directives.
